title: Initial position: pawn moves
description: Standard starting position. With a Pawn (1) rolled, any white pawn can advance one or two squares forward.

title: King only under Rook roll
description: The active player only has a King on the board. With a Rook (4) rolled, there are no rooks to move, and the King cannot act as a Rook. No legal moves are generated.

title: King on the first row
description: The King is located on e1 with no other pieces on the board. Under a King (6) roll, all standard adjacent moves are legal.

title: Queen moves from f3
description: The Queen is on f3 in a semi-open board. Under a Queen (5) roll, it can move along any unobstructed diagonal, rank, or file.

title: En Passant and Path Blockage
description: A complex pawn scenario: the pawn on e5 can capture the black d5 pawn en passant (exd6). The c2 pawn's two-square advance is blocked by the bishop on c4, so only c2-c3 is legal. The f2 pawn is completely blocked by the queen on f3.

title: En Passant, Pawn Choices, and Forward Blockage
description: The C pawn can either capture or move forward one square. The D pawn can move one or two squares forward, exposing the white king to check. The E pawn can capture en passant or take the knight, but cannot move forward.

title: Pawn promotion
description: A pawn on the seventh rank can advance to the eighth rank and promote to any piece (usually a queen). In this case, with a roll of 1, the only legal move is d7d8, which results in a pawn promotion.

title: King and pawn, king move
description: A pawn on the seventh rank can advance to the eighth rank and promote to any piece (usually a queen). In this case, with a roll of 6, the king moves.

title: Pawn promotion or capture
description: With a roll of Pawn (1), the pawn on d7 can promote to any piece on d8, or capture the black king on e8.
